div1.entryFree:
Formal, 1) regular, orderly, accurate, according to rule and custom: her hair, nor loose, nor tied in f. plat, Compl. 29. beard of f. cut, As II, 7, 155. the f. vice Iniquity, R3 III, 1, 82 (== customary).* no noble right nor f. ostentation, Hml. IV, 5, 215. to make of him a f. man again, Err. V, 105 (i. e. an ordinary man, a man in his senses; cf. Informal). this is evident to any f. capacity, Tw. II, 5, 128. thou shouldst come like a Fury crowned with snakes, not like a f. man, Ant. II, 5, 41 (== common, ordinary).
2) precise, pedantic: are you so f.? Shr. III, 1, 61. f. in apparel, IV, 2, 64.
3) grave, dignified: and flow henceforth in f. majesty, H4B V, 2, 133. with untired spirits and f. constancy, Caes. II, 1, 227.
